sources.label: Investing.com - Maytronics Q4 2025 earnings call transcript (strategic shift)
Globes - Profit warning sends Maytronics to new low
CTech - Maytronics looking to resurface after deep dive in value

verdicts.rankRationale: No material news since June 6 and a -0.7% drift day; yesterday's hold stands. The -39.8% YTD is driven by a genuine fundamentals breakdown (FY2025 net loss of 222M ILS, revenue -13.6%, gross margin 26.7% vs 36%), so the sell-off does not cleanly convert into improved alpha asymmetry on a robotics-scaling list.
verdicts.bull: New CEO Rafael Benami's pivot to core robotic pool cleaning, exit from non-core water monitoring, and the Dolphin EON launch give a credible turnaround path; operating cash flow already surged 126% to 292M ILS, and the name is washed-out and under-owned at these levels.
verdicts.neutral: Turnaround signals (CEO pivot, EON launch, leadership reshuffle) all predate June 6 and are already in yesterday's case; until H1 2026 numbers show margin repair, the stock is a deep-value consumer-robotics option, peripheral to the AI-compute bottleneck core.
verdicts.bear: Consumer-discretionary exposure with currency volatility, U.S. tariff risk, and North American weather sensitivity; the loss was a profit-warning-grade fundamental break, and Chinese competition (e.g., Chasing, settled via patent deal) keeps pressuring the Dolphin franchise's pricing power.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: H1 2026 results showing gross-margin recovery and early traction of the streamlined robotics-only portfolio under the new CEO.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Continued revenue erosion from Chinese pool-robot entrants plus tariff and FX headwinds turning the 2025 loss into a structural decline rather than a cyclical trough.
